channel.title: Epic Fury Live — US-Iran War Tracker
channel.description: Live tracking of the US-Israel-Iran conflict. Real-time updates, verified casualties, evidence, and analysis.

item.title: Analysts say Trump doesn’t understand Iran reality has changed
item.description: Watch this discussion between Ross Harrison and Hassan Ahmadian on US strategy regarding Iran.

item.title: Projectile from Lebanon kills at least one in north Israel
item.description: Video captured flames and black smoke where a projectile from Lebanon killed at least one person in northern Israel.

item.title: Iran war live: Trump delays attacks on Iranian energy sector by 10 days
item.description: Iranian missiles, drones target Kuwait, UAE, Saudi Arabia and Jordan as Trump says Iran has 'chance to make a deal'.

item.title: Trump ‘pausing’ plan to destroy Iranian power plants for 10 days
item.description: Donald Trump says he is “pausing” his plan to destroy Iranian power plants for 10 days.

item.title: EU’s top diplomat urges US to pressure Russia to stop aiding Iran
item.description: The European Union’s Foreign Policy Chief Kaja Kallas says the US needs to pressure Russia to stop assisting Iran.

item.title: Manila’s streets empty as fuel prices surge amid Strait of Hormuz crisis
item.description: Empty streets expose another crisis as Filipinos battle soaring oil prices, dwindling incomes, and a stagnating economy.

item.title: Why Iraq is the most fragile front in the US-Israel war on Iran
item.description: Iraq is under attack from all sides as militias and foreign powers clash.

item.title: What are the red lines for Iran and the US in the war?
item.description: Tehran says US list of 15 demands does not reflect reality.

item.title: Trump postpones US strikes on Iranian power grid to April 6 amid talks
item.description: The US president has threatened the attacks as a means of pressuring Iran to reopen Strait of Hormuz, a vital waterway.

item.title: Al Jazeera visits Iran’s UNESCO-listed palace damaged by war
item.description: Al Jazeera visited Tehran's UNESCO-listed Golestan Palace, which Iran says was damaged by US-Israeli strikes.
